scaled_oracle_cases.description: Normal operation with valid file and typical params. Tests that function constructs correct command and returns expected outputs when all validations pass.
scaled_oracle_cases.semantic_intent: Function must validate file existence, no duplicate flags, no shell metacharacters, no conflicting built-in flags, and produce correct command for valid inputs.
scaled_oracle_cases.target_constraint: Function must raise no exceptions for valid inputs and produce command list with deblur workflow plus flags.
scaled_oracle_cases.expected_failure_mode: Old seed solution would not check file existence or other validations, but it would still produce correct command. New solution must include those checks but still work for valid case.
scaled_oracle_cases.setup_code:
logging.basicConfig(level=logging.DEBUG)
input_fp = 'test_sequences.fasta'
with open(input_fp, 'w') as f:
    f.write(">seq1
ACTG
>seq2
TGCA
")
covered_requirements: Scaled executable cases must include tests for normal, empty, special characters, and multiple params scenarios.
Scaled executable cases must include a test for non-existent input file expecting FileNotFoundError.
Scaled executable cases must include a test for duplicate flags expecting ValueError.
Scaled executable cases must include a test for shell metacharacters expecting ValueError.
Scaled executable cases must include a test for conflicting built-in flags expecting ValueError.
Scaled executable cases must include exception tests for adversarial constraints.

scaled_oracle_cases.description: Test with empty params list and valid file. Expects command with only built-in flags.
scaled_oracle_cases.semantic_intent: Function works correctly with empty params.
scaled_oracle_cases.target_constraint: Function must produce correct command when params is empty.
scaled_oracle_cases.expected_failure_mode: If function fails to handle empty list, it may error or produce wrong command.
scaled_oracle_cases.setup_code:
logging.basicConfig(level=logging.DEBUG)
input_fp = 'test.fasta'
with open(input_fp, 'w') as f:
    f.write(">seq1\nACTG\n")
covered_requirements: Scaled executable cases must include tests for normal, empty, special characters, and multiple params scenarios.

scaled_oracle_cases.description: Test with input_fp containing spaces and parentheses (no shell metacharacters). Expects command quoting the path appropriately.
scaled_oracle_cases.semantic_intent: Function handles special characters in file path (spaces) gracefully.
scaled_oracle_cases.target_constraint: Function must include the path as a single list element without breaking the command.
scaled_oracle_cases.expected_failure_mode: If function does not quote or escapes incorrectly, command may be malformed.
scaled_oracle_cases.setup_code:
logging.basicConfig(level=logging.DEBUG)
input_fp = 'my sequences (1).fasta'
with open(input_fp, 'w') as f:
    f.write(">seq\nACTG\n")
covered_requirements: Scaled executable cases must include tests for normal, empty, special characters, and multiple params scenarios.

scaled_oracle_cases.description: Test with non-existent input file. Expects FileNotFoundError.
scaled_oracle_cases.semantic_intent: Function validates input file existence and raises FileNotFoundError if missing.
scaled_oracle_cases.target_constraint: Function must raise FileNotFoundError when input_fp does not exist.
scaled_oracle_cases.expected_failure_mode: Old seed solution does not check file existence, so it would not raise an error.
scaled_oracle_cases.assertion_code: assert result['exception'] == 'FileNotFoundError', f"Expected FileNotFoundError but got {result['exception']}"
covered_requirements: Scaled executable cases must include a test for non-existent input file expecting FileNotFoundError.

scaled_oracle_cases.description: Test with duplicate '--min-size' flag in params. Expects ValueError.
scaled_oracle_cases.semantic_intent: Function rejects duplicate flags in params.
scaled_oracle_cases.target_constraint: Function must raise ValueError when params contains duplicate flags.
scaled_oracle_cases.expected_failure_mode: Old seed solution does not check duplicates, so it would not raise an error.
scaled_oracle_cases.setup_code:
logging.basicConfig(level=logging.DEBUG)
input_fp = 'test.fasta'
with open(input_fp, 'w') as f:
    f.write(">seq\nACGT\n")
scaled_oracle_cases.assertion_code: assert result['exception'] == 'ValueError', f"Expected ValueError but got {result['exception']}"
covered_requirements: Scaled executable cases must include a test for duplicate flags expecting ValueError.

scaled_oracle_cases.description: Test with input_fp containing semicolon. Expects ValueError for shell metacharacter.
scaled_oracle_cases.semantic_intent: Function prevents command injection via input_fp.
scaled_oracle_cases.target_constraint: Function must raise ValueError when input_fp contains shell metacharacters.
scaled_oracle_cases.expected_failure_mode: Old seed solution does not sanitize, so it would not raise an error.
scaled_oracle_cases.assertion_code: assert result['exception'] == 'ValueError', f"Expected ValueError but got {result['exception']}"
covered_requirements: Scaled executable cases must include a test for shell metacharacters expecting ValueError.

scaled_oracle_cases.description: Test with params containing command substitution. Expects ValueError for shell metacharacter.
scaled_oracle_cases.semantic_intent: Function prevents command injection via params.
scaled_oracle_cases.target_constraint: Function must raise ValueError when params contains shell metacharacters.
scaled_oracle_cases.expected_failure_mode: Old seed solution does not sanitize, so it would not raise an error.
scaled_oracle_cases.setup_code:
logging.basicConfig(level=logging.DEBUG)
input_fp = 'test.fasta'
with open(input_fp, 'w') as f:
    f.write(">seq\nACGT\n")
scaled_oracle_cases.assertion_code: assert result['exception'] == 'ValueError', f"Expected ValueError but got {result['exception']}"
covered_requirements: Scaled executable cases must include a test for shell metacharacters expecting ValueError.

scaled_oracle_cases.description: Test with params containing '--seqs-fp' flag. Expects ValueError for conflicting built-in flag.
scaled_oracle_cases.semantic_intent: Function rejects params that include flags automatically added by the function.
scaled_oracle_cases.target_constraint: Function must raise ValueError when params contains '--seqs-fp' flag.
scaled_oracle_cases.expected_failure_mode: Old seed solution would append it, causing duplicate flags; new solution must detect and raise.
scaled_oracle_cases.setup_code:
logging.basicConfig(level=logging.DEBUG)
input_fp = 'test.fasta'
with open(input_fp, 'w') as f:
    f.write(">seq\nACGT\n")
scaled_oracle_cases.assertion_code: assert result['exception'] == 'ValueError', f"Expected ValueError but got {result['exception']}"
covered_requirements: Scaled executable cases must include a test for conflicting built-in flags expecting ValueError.
